what_it_does: Acceptance test for the storage-discovery / run-index layer. It seeds and live-runs generate chains, projects the existing "op.run" event log into discoverable run:// rows via Suite.list_runs/find_runs, demonstrates feeding a discovered run:// address back into a later run as input, and verifies the MCP tool wrappers, additive emit behavior, and governance-floor constraints.
observations: Sets an isolated per-process COMPANY_STORE under /tmp before importing the server so module-level SUITE binds to a clean store.
Probes 127.0.0.1:8000 to decide whether live engine legs run or are skipped.
Seeds a synthetic "op.run" record to test the list_runs/find_runs projection without a live model.
Uses _cog.run_items with srv._cog_emit to exercise the real engine path when the brain is up.
Uses _cog.resolve_address to materialize a discovered run:// output before feeding it downstream.
Tests named-input address resolution through srv.run_role(inputs={"live_state": discovered}).
Verifies run_items and run_reduce signatures remain unchanged (additive emit inside).
Simulates a partially failed fan and checks that only successfully set_ref'd run:// addresses appear in the op.run index.
Checks that every indexed op.run record is an engine run-op and never a resolve/approve/dispatch governance verb.
standouts: The outputs→inputs-by-discovery loop: discover a run:// address from the projected index and feed it into a subsequent run.
The dual model-free seeded path plus conditional live path against a resident 4B model.
The governance-floor assertion that op.run telemetry records are never operator verbs.
conventions: Mirrors mcp_engine by binding module-level SUITE at import time.
Uses a global PASS counter and a simple check(label, cond) helper instead of a test framework.
Performs conditional test execution based on an external brain service.
Manipulates the store directly to seed records and simulate failure cases.
concerns: Relies on global mutable state (SUITE, store, environment) and import-order side effects.
Live legs are skipped silently when the brain is down, so the file is not fully deterministic.
The failure-simulation block catches a bare Exception, which could hide unexpected errors.
The check helper only increments a counter; failures are asserted but not grouped by test case.
notes: A verify-by-use acceptance test that closes the loop from emitting run records to discovering and reusing run:// addresses, with graceful degradation when the local model is unavailable.
questions: What exact fields does the "op.run" event emitted by srv._cog_emit contain?
Which operations are included in SUITE.ENGINE_RUN_OPS and how is that set maintained?
How does find_runs map its query parameters onto the fields of an op.run record?
What mechanism guarantees that a failed unit's run:// address is never set_ref'd?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it proves the op.run run-index, the list_runs/find_runs read-time projection, the outputs→inputs composability loop, and the MCP discovery tools.
apparent_themes: Verify-by-use with real engine and graceful degradation
Governance floor: telemetry records must not be operator verbs
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that run outputs become first-class discoverable inputs, that the discovery index is a cheap projection over existing telemetry, and that the change is additive and safe for governance.
novelty: An acceptance test that closes the full discovery loop—from op.run emit to projected index to feeding a discovered address back into a new run—using both seeded model-free and conditional live paths.
contribution: A runnable acceptance test that validates the run-discovery substrate: op.run indexing, Suite and MCP list_runs/find_runs projections, the outputs→inputs composability loop, and additive/governance-floor invariants.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that seeds and live-runs generate chains to validate a run:// discovery index projected from op.run events, then feeds discovered addresses back into downstream runs while checking MCP tooling and governance-floor constraints.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that run outputs are discoverable as inputs via a read-time projection over existing op.run telemetry without changing store semantics or introducing governance verbs.

symbols.does: A tiny test helper that asserts a condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts cond is truthy, raising with the label on failure; on success it increments the global PASS counter and prints an 'ok' line. It is the file's minimal assertion harness.

symbols.does: Probes the resident model server at 127.0.0.1:8000 to decide whether live engine legs can run.
symbols.description: _brain_up() attempts a short HTTP GET to /v1/models on the local 4B service and returns True if reachable. The result is stored in BRAIN and used to skip or run the live generate-chain and MCP-run_role sections.
